skill.name: nano-banana
skill.description: Generate images with Google Gemini native image models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Gemini 3 Pro Image, Gemini 2.5 Flash Image. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input. Triggers: nano banana, gemini image, gemini 3 pro image, gemini 2.5 flash image, google image generation, native image generation, gemini native image
skill.summary: Generate images with Google Gemini native image models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Gemini 3 Pro Im...
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Static command findings are mostly legitimate CLI examples for running inference.sh image apps, but the skill authorizes external CLI use and networked image processing. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives from descriptive text and table content, with no evidence of cryptographic code or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Networked External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to run infsh commands that contact inference.sh and Google Gemini image services. This is expected functionality, but prompts and input image URLs may be sent to external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples directly invoke the infsh CLI against named Gemini image apps. This confirms external service use, although it matches the skill purpose and is not evidence of malicious activity.

medium_findings.title: External Skill Installation Guidance
medium_findings.description: The instructions recommend npx skills add commands for installing inference.sh related skills. This creates supply chain exposure if users run the commands without checking the source.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The install commands are explicit and network-dependent. The commands appear legitimate for this ecosystem, so the risk is supply chain exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Optional Real-Time Search Grounding
medium_findings.description: The example enables Google Search grounding for an image generation request. This expands network access and can send prompt context to additional external services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The enable_google_search option is shown directly in the sample input. The behavior is documented as real-time grounding, but the exact downstream data handling depends on the service.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation and Example URLs
low_findings.description: The skill contains hardcoded URLs for documentation, an image, and an example image input. These support the skill documentation and do not show exfiltration or hidden callbacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links or sample inputs. I found no evidence that secrets or local files are sent to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the referenced lines contain descriptive text, prompt examples, or a parameter table. No cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found no hash, cipher, or password handling code. The pattern match is not semantically related to cryptography.

dangerous_patterns.title: Broad infsh Command Permission
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill declares Bash access for infsh commands. This is required for the skill, but it allows networked CLI operations through that tool.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration is explicit. The risk is constrained to infsh commands, so it is elevated but not a confirmed exploit.

content.value_statement: Image generation workflows often require model-specific commands and input formats. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code direct prompts for Gemini native image apps through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to run Gemini image generation apps with the infsh CLI.
Provides examples for text-to-image prompts and multi-image output.
Documents image editing with input image URLs.
Lists supported options such as aspect ratio, resolution, output format, and Google Search grounding.
Compares Gemini 3 Pro Image and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image for speed and quality.
Links to inference.sh documentation for app running, streaming, and file handling.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI and an authenticated account.
Sends prompts and image inputs to external model services.
Does not include local image generation or offline processing.
Model availability, pricing, and output limits depend on inference.sh and Google Gemini services.

use_cases.title: Create campaign visuals
use_cases.description: Generate several visual directions from a marketing prompt, then refine aspect ratio and style for each channel.

use_cases.title: Prototype product imagery
use_cases.description: Use Gemini image prompts to explore product scenes, textures, and lighting before commissioning final assets.

use_cases.title: Edit reference images
use_cases.description: Provide an input image URL and request targeted changes such as added objects, mood changes, or composition updates.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate one image from a short description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to create a square photorealistic image of a modern desk setup with natural morning light.

prompt_templates.title: Styled series
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate multiple options in a consistent style.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana with Gemini 2.5 Flash Image to create four minimalist logo concepts for a coffee shop. Keep the palette warm and simple.

prompt_templates.title: Edited image
prompt_templates.scenario: Modify an existing image using a URL.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to edit this image URL: [image URL]. Add a soft rainbow in the sky and keep the original landscape realistic.

prompt_templates.title: Grounded visual brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Create an image from current information and specific output settings.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana with Gemini 3 Pro Image, 16:9 aspect ratio, and Google Search grounding to visualize current weather in Tokyo as an editorial illustration.

output_examples.input: Create a 16:9 panoramic mountain landscape with northern lights.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares an inference.sh Gemini image request with a wide aspect ratio and a detailed landscape prompt.

output_examples.input: Generate four logo options for a coffee shop.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects the faster Gemini image app, requests four images, and keeps the prompt focused on logo style and brand mood.

output_examples.input: Add a rainbow to this landscape image URL.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares an image editing request that preserves the source landscape while adding the requested sky detail.

best_practices: Review prompts and image URLs before sending them to external services.
Use Gemini 2.5 Flash Image for quick drafts and Gemini 3 Pro Image for higher quality outputs.
Specify aspect ratio, style, lighting, and key details in the initial prompt.
anti_patterns: Do not send confidential images or private customer data unless your policy allows it.
Do not run installation commands from community skills without reviewing the source.
Do not enable Google Search grounding when current information is not needed.

faq.question: Does this skill generate images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the inference.sh CLI to call hosted Gemini image models.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents image editing through input image URLs.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs an external CLI and sends content to network services.

faq.question: Are the weak cryptography findings confirmed?
faq.answer: No. Manual review found no cryptographic code on the cited lines.

faq.question: What should users check before using it?
faq.answer: Users should verify the inference.sh CLI, account permissions, costs, and data handling rules.
